next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 2

By noon the city had his joke in its teeth.

Silas climbed Lantern Steps through air that smelled of wet lime, lamp smoke, and the sweet warm rot the Sallow River breathed into every lower street after rain. He had no clean reason to be there. A sensible man would have stayed on Patent Row, taken another lap past the windows where people were already pointing at last night's pink bloom, and let the word he'd flung off a signboard do what words did in Nacre Bend, which was travel farther than their author deserved and pick up worse company on the way. But sense had never kept him in tobacco or paid Havelock Pigments on time. Curiosity had done better. Vanity had done best of all. He wanted to see whether bridelight had truly climbed into the stair district or whether the morning's talk had only painted rumor over damp stone.

It had climbed.

Every third public lamp along the rail showed some stain of it. Not a steady color, not yet. A blush under the green, a watered rose caught in the glass, the sort of tint a man could call charming if he had no intention of carrying soup, babies, or his own half-drunk body down slick steps after dark. A fishwoman in a tarred apron laughed the word to her sister, and the lamp over their heads flushed a shade brighter as if it liked applause. Two boys half his nephew's age had pink chalk on their cuffs and were arguing over who had first heard him say it. A clerk from Gilt Crescent, clean collar already losing the fight with humidity, had come down only to stare and say the name with the reverence some men reserved for a new r...
open_hooks_to_carry: Establish Silas as a genuinely gifted but morally slippery sign painter whose mouth is part performance and part livelihood.
Show Nacre Bend's naming economy: products, pests, and strains gain value when the right phrase catches.
state_deltas_required: Patent Row expects Silas to keep generating profitable names while the runaway moss demands an answer in public.
Silas's offhand christening of the glow-moss becomes a citywide phenomenon, turning his verbal swagger into material consequence.
